# Questions in Livestream 183

Live Stream #183: Happy All Saints Day!

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/UnLfr0rCC2k?t=585" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">9:45</a> | Does Isaiah 21 linking Tema with Arabia prove that Arabs are Ishmaelites? | He said no. The place name preserves an older ethnographic memory, but that does not mean the later inhabitants were still Ishmaelites. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/UnLfr0rCC2k?t=822" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">13:42</a> | How does he effectively retain what he reads? | He says he reads a lot, lets repeated exposure reinforce content, and recommends reading over elaborate note systems. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/UnLfr0rCC2k?t=1022" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">17:02</a> | What if the book is a library book and cannot be marked? | He says to read it anyway and take notes separately if needed. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/UnLfr0rCC2k?t=1212" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">20:12</a> | What should parenting look like in Christian terms? | He emphasized teaching the faith, discipline, and example, while warning against trying to dictate every life choice. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/UnLfr0rCC2k?t=1988" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">33:08</a> | What are his thoughts on Kip's community post? | He criticizes the post as evasive and immature, especially for not naming him while attacking his arguments. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/UnLfr0rCC2k?t=2562" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">42:42</a> | Is there a book with a parallel translation of the Amarna letters? | He is not sure about a parallel edition, but recommends translations by Moran and Anson Rainey. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/UnLfr0rCC2k?t=2954" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">49:14</a> | Could the story of Jonah support the idea of postmortem salvation? | He says Jonah may suggest a special exception in God's providence, but not a normal pattern. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/UnLfr0rCC2k?t=3554" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">59:14</a> | Where does the tradition come from that Salmon was one of the spies at Jericho? | He said it does not come from the biblical genealogies themselves and is more likely from later rabbinic or Second Temple tradition. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/UnLfr0rCC2k?t=5215" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:26:55</a> | Does Genesis 30:25-26 imply Jacob was Laban's slave? | He says the situation is more complicated and works through Jacob's service arrangement with Laban. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/UnLfr0rCC2k?t=6225" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:43:45</a> | Without the Ark of the Covenant, what was in the Holy of Holies, and is that why the veil was torn? | He says the ark functioned as a vehicle of sacred space, but its absence is not the main reason for the torn veil. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/UnLfr0rCC2k?t=7305" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:01:45</a> | Did he write today? | Yes. He says he wrote and programmed while preparing the first novel for press. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/UnLfr0rCC2k?t=8945" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:29:05</a> | Were the Israelites enslaved during the Iron Age? | No. He placed the enslavement earlier, in the Bronze Age, tied to events around Avaris and the Hyksos period. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/UnLfr0rCC2k?t=9275" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:34:35</a> | Did Jews take Canaanite divine-council beliefs and convert them into angels? | He distinguishes shared Canaanite background from direct Ugaritic influence on Judaism. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/UnLfr0rCC2k?t=9610" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:40:10</a> | If the flood waters receded by wind, does that mean not every inch of earth was covered? | He says water cannot recede if it is everywhere and notes the debate over global versus local flood language. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/UnLfr0rCC2k?t=9890" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:44:50</a> | If a father falls into vice, is the son likely to follow because it is hereditary? | He rejected a simple hereditary explanation and stressed influence, discipline, and environment instead. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/UnLfr0rCC2k?t=10191" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:49:51</a> | What made pre-Christian Egyptian society amenable to Christianity spreading there? | He points to late changes in Egyptian religion and related cultural conditions that made Christianity more plausible in Egypt. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/UnLfr0rCC2k?t=10582" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:56:22</a> | Is it biblically right to marry a second cousin? | He says he does not know of a biblical prohibition, but advises against it for practical reasons. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/UnLfr0rCC2k?t=12465" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:27:45</a> | Why do some clan numbers in Numbers change while the number of clans is mostly preserved? | He says the point is continuity and preservation of the clan structure, not zero change. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/UnLfr0rCC2k?t=12941" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:35:41</a> | Do population estimates for Egypt and Canaan refute a literal 600,000 Israelites? | He says it is not merely an argument from silence; the issue is how to interpret the census figures alongside population evidence. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/UnLfr0rCC2k?t=13492" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:44:52</a> | Can he comment on representing Raphael Greenberg in response to Rationality Rules? | He says he would need to look closer, but he does not think Greenberg was misquoted in the same way as another scholar. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/UnLfr0rCC2k?t=14460" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:01:00</a> | Is there evidence inside the Hebrew Bible for changes from one phase of Hebrew to another that can help date books? | Yes. He pointed to lexicography and other language shifts as one of the tools scholars use for relative dating. |
